calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-03-2023 Vacated

MOTION FOR WRIT OF ATTACHMENT - Notice Of Application And Hearing For Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Sep-09-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. TODD JANSSEN, Esq. for Law Office of Thomas F. Nowland (Tel: 949-221-0005) for defendant Indinero Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ZENDESK, INC.'s Application And Hearing For Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion is denied.

As background, we recall this: "The Attachment Law, which authorizes "a harsh remedy [that] ... causes the defendant to lose control of his property before the plaintiff's claim is adjudicated" [citation] is generally construed strictly according to the letter of its statutory terms." Royals v. Lu, 81 Cal. App. 5th 328, 296 Cal. Rptr. 3d 854, 865 (2022); see also e.g., Pac. Decision Scis. Corp. v. Superior Ct., 121 Cal. App. 4th 1100, 1106 (2004).
There are many defects in the application, most of them individually sufficient to deny the application. These are some of the defects:
" The court cannot find probable validity of the claims, because (1) it is not clear there is an operative contract: no signed contract was appended to the papers; (2) defendant attempted to cancel its obligations and may have actually done so, and thus the scope of its obligation is unclear (thus, also, the amount is not readily ascertainable);
" There is no contract claim in the complaint. There is apparently an open book account claim, but that's not viable absent a contract. Eloquence Corp. v. Home Consignment Ctr., 49 Cal. App. 5th 655, 665 (2020). A memorandum of points and authorities might have clarified the relationship between the pleaded claims and the contract requirement for a writ of attachment;
" The wrong box was checked on the application - 9c which is for natural persons, but the defendant here is not a natural person;
" There appears to be no mention in the moving papers of an undertaking (bond). Compare, e.g., Vershbow v. Reiner, 231 Cal. App. 3d 879, 882 (1991);
" There is no moving memorandum of points and authorities, required by CRC 3.1113. Thus I conclude the application has no merit. CRC 3.1113 (a).
See generally, Weil & Brown, et al., CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL 9:853 ff. (Rutter: 2022).

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/CK)
